_comment: Perhaps* 24-Point Source Integrity Check — HTML Generator Input Schema v1.0
_instructions: One JSON file per company. Fill every field. Use null for optional fields not applicable. Verdict values: PASS | FLAG | FAIL | N/A. Regulatory items go first in exec_issues list (set reg_flag: true).

meta.company_name: string — display name e.g. AegisIQ Equity AI

exec_issues._comment: One object per flagged/failed issue. Order: regulatory items first (reg_flag: true), then substantiation gaps.
exec_issues.title: string — short issue title e.g. Traceability & link integrity
exec_issues.resolution: string — content fix only, no legal conclusion
exec_issues.reg_flag: boolean — true if regulatory/compliance scrutinized category
exec_issues.custody_predicate: boolean — at most one per report. True marks the issue that names the unified absence of a human review authority, an external verification process, and a tamper-evident generation record. When one issue carries true, it renders full in the Executive Summary modal and all other exec_issues render as single-line stub pointers to their section tabs. When no issue carries true, the modal renders unchanged.

sections.title: string — e.g. Source Chain-of-Custody
sections.overall_verdict: string — mixed | pass | flag | fail | na
sections.triage_label: string — e.g. Read first — Regulatory / compliance | Flags | Flag | null
sections.triage_is_reg: boolean — true if triage_label is regulatory
sections.clean_line: string — the ✔ clean summary or null if no clean items
